items.violence_logic: Preservation, sacrificial obligation, providential judgment, reconciliation
items.enemy_as_bringer_of_death: Weak in the reviewed pilot evidence; disunion/slavery are threats, but enemy agency is often muted or diffused.
items.death_logic: Soldier death becomes obligation, devotion, and condition for national renewal.
items.historical_alignment: Battlefield death, public mourning, emancipation context, wartime bloodshed, and reunion practice require publication citations before final claims.
items.guilt_structure: Shared national guilt and providential judgment, especially in the Second Inaugural.
items.endpoint: Reunion, democratic survival, reconciliation, and renewal.
items.claim_boundary: Lincoln is comparison-ready only as a provisional pilot case; full-corpus review and reliability adjudication remain pending.

items.sacred_collective_object: Aryan Volk, blood-and-soil nation, racial purity
items.dominant_body_metaphor: Racial body, Volk as organic collective, parasite/pathogen (enemy)
items.violence_logic: Racial purification, extermination of contaminating enemy, national rebirth through blood
items.enemy_as_bringer_of_death: Strong — Jewish/Bolshevik enemy is explicit death-bearer and existential contamination threat.
items.death_logic: Death of enemies required for collective survival; German soldier deaths are sacrifice for racial Volk.
items.historical_alignment: Nuremberg Laws 1935, Operation Barbarossa 1941, Wannsee 1942 industrial genocide, documented social uptake in Nazi state machinery.
items.guilt_structure: External guilt projected onto racial enemy; no internal moral accounting. Contamination logic inverts guilt.
items.endpoint: Racial state, Lebensraum, extermination of designated enemies, Thousand-Year Reich.
items.claim_boundary: Hitler case is draft-scored; full-corpus German source review and reliability adjudication remain pending.

items.sacred_collective_object: La Gloire, l'Empire, la France — imperial glory as sacred collective object
items.dominant_body_metaphor: Army-as-body, Emperor as embodiment of the nation
items.violence_logic: Military glory, conquest as historical destiny, soldier sacrifice for the Emperor and Empire
items.enemy_as_bringer_of_death: Weak to moderate — enemies are obstacles to order; death-bearer framing clearest in 29th Bulletin.
items.death_logic: Soldier death converted to honor and sacrifice for glory; Eylau is the clearest expression.
items.historical_alignment: Austerlitz mobilization, Eylau mass casualties, 29th Bulletin catastrophe acknowledgment corroborated historically.
items.guilt_structure: Minimal — bulletins externalize loss onto weather, terrain, or enemy resistance; no shared-guilt logic.
items.endpoint: Imperial consolidation, Napoleonic peace, historical glory; not extermination or republican founding.
items.claim_boundary: Napoleon case is draft-scored from bulletin corpus; thin evidence base (19 instances) and genre constraints limit generalization.

items.sacred_collective_object: Liberty, republican self-governance, natural rights, Providence-backed founding
items.dominant_body_metaphor: Body politic, patriot sacrifice, civic organism
items.violence_logic: Defensive founding violence, tyranny-resistance as sacred obligation, providential justification
items.enemy_as_bringer_of_death: Moderate — British tyranny and Hessian mercenaries as death-bearers; tyranny as slavery-and-death frame.
items.death_logic: Patriot death as founding sacrifice; blood of martyrs consecrates the republic.
items.historical_alignment: Continental Army mobilization, Battle of Trenton, Valley Forge sacrifice, documented pamphlet reception corroborated.
items.guilt_structure: External guilt projected onto Crown and loyalists; colonists positioned as innocent victims of tyranny.
items.endpoint: Republican founding, constitutional self-governance, permanent severance from monarchical authority.
items.claim_boundary: Am-Rev case is draft-scored; tech debt #42 means am-rev MIPVU pipeline may have overwritten reviewed annotations for Washington Orders documents.

guardrails: Structural comparison is not moral equivalence.
Similarity in metaphor form is not equivalence in political content, institutional setting, or historical outcome.
Enemy construction, endpoint, violence logic, and historical enactment matter centrally.
Each case remains historically contextualized and evidence-bound.
Comparator cases without support ratings remain pending, not inferred by analogy.
War, genocide, enslavement, colonial violence, and state terror require separate historical and moral description even when symbolic structures overlap.
